key_departures.impact: Released with a post-June 1 designation, carrying $47.5M in dead cap in 2026 and $7.2M in 2027. Murray was the franchise QB for 7 seasons but struggled with injuries and inconsistency in 2025, replaced by Jacoby Brissett. Cardinals now have no long-term QB solution — Brissett and Minshew are bridge options only.

Signed a 3yr/$33M deal (up to $36M) with Dallas. Thompson was a cornerstone of Arizona's secondary for 7 seasons, starting alongside Budda Baker. His departure leaves a significant hole at safety — Dadrion Taylor-Demerson projects as his replacement but is unproven as a full-time starter.

Released after just one season of a 2yr/$29M deal. Saved $9.4M in cap space. Started all 17 games but only produced 1 sack and 26 tackles. Departure leaves a hole at IDL but low production meant the need was already existing.
Released along with Akeem Davis-Gaither for over $10M in combined cap savings. Rotational DL piece; departure further thins an already thin defensive interior.
Released alongside Nichols. Was a rotational linebacker; departure creates minor depth concern at LB but not a critical starter loss.

Released on March 12, freeing $4.9M in cap space. Was the starting left guard, graded 61st of 81 eligible guards per PFF. Departure is mitigated by the signing of Isaac Seumalo to take his spot at LG.

Became an unrestricted free agent. Was the starting RG in 2025 but had a blown block rate over 4% per SIS and was injury-prone. Departure creates a RG void, partially addressed by Seumalo, Pryor, and Wilkinson.
Re-signed on a one-year deal — departed as UFA but returned. Net neutral on OT need.

Became a free agent entering his age-40 season. Had 6.5 sacks in 2025 in a rotational role but is at end of career. Departure is an expected transition; IDL need escalation is absorbed by Lopez and Collier re-signings.

signings.analysis: The headline IOL signing of the offseason for Arizona. Pro Bowl guard with 73.3 PFF grade in 2025, slots in as the starting LG after Evan Brown's release. 3yr/$31.5M with $19M guaranteed is a genuine multi-year commitment at a starting tier AAV. However, Seumalo is 32 and dealt with two injuries in 2025, and the RG spot (vacated by Will Hernandez) remains unresolved beyond Matt Pryor/Isaiah Adams competition. IOL goes from critical tier to reduced need but is not fully addressed — they still need a starting-caliber RG or OT via draft.

Returns to Arizona after one season in Detroit where he posted 2 sacks, 4 TFLs, 30 tackles in 17 games. 2yr/$11.5M with $7M guaranteed signals a genuine 2-year investment for a starting-level role, absorbing snaps vacated by Tomlinson. At $5.75M AAV, this is below the starter threshold for DT by positional market standards, but the 2-year commitment and $7M guarantees indicate he is the team's expected IDL1. Still a rotational/run-stuffer profile rather than a pass rush threat — IDL need is reduced but not fully resolved, especially with Calais Campbell gone.

Bourne brings veteran experience and familiarity with HC Mike LaFleur from their shared time in San Francisco. Caught 37 passes for 551 yards in 2025. At ~$5.7M AAV on a 2-year deal (AP reported up to $12M with incentives), this is a depth-tier WR signing. He fills the WR3 role the Cardinals lacked in 2025, but does not solve the need for an elite WR2 opposite Marvin Harrison Jr. WR need slightly reduced — depth/role player addition, not a starter-level investment.

Adds quality RB depth behind James Conner and Trey Benson. Allgeier rushed for 1,000 yards as a rookie and is a downhill runner well suited for short-yardage/red zone situations. At $6.1M AAV on a 2-year deal, this is starter-tier money for the RB market, but he projects as RB2/3 in Arizona behind Conner (restructured) and Benson. RB was not a top need — this is a depth enhancement, not a need resolution.

One-year, $5.75M deal (up to $8M with incentives). Minshew is expected to back up Jacoby Brissett, who remains the QB1. Coming off a fractured tibia in KC in 2025, Minshew is a veteran bridge option at best. The Cardinals hold the No. 3 overall pick and are widely expected to select a franchise QB in the 2026 draft — Minshew/Brissett are placeholder options. The 1-year deal is an explicit signal this does not address the long-term QB need at all. QB remains a critical tier 1 draft priority.

One-year, $3M base deal (up to $4M) signed to address the Jalen Thompson departure. Wingard started 16 games for Jacksonville in 2025, recording 84 tackles and 1 INT, but PFF ranked him 72nd of 91 safeties. He projects as a depth/competition piece alongside Dadrion Taylor-Demerson next to Budda Baker. The 1-year deal and below-starter money confirm this is not a true Thompson replacement — S remains a genuine need for the draft.

One-year deal (terms undisclosed, expected near minimum/depth level). Versatile lineman who can play guard or tackle. Adds depth/competition at RG after Will Hernandez's departure. Started 1 game in 2025 for PHI (was starting RG in Chicago in 2024). Primarily a depth move — does not address the RG starter need meaningfully. Pushes Isaiah Adams for the starting RG job.

Returns to Arizona on a 2-year deal (financial terms not yet reported). Started all 17 games at RT for the Falcons in 2025 on an OL that allowed the 4th-fewest sacks. Projects as the Cardinals' starting RT opposite Paris Johnson Jr., filling the void left by Jonah Williams/Kelvin Beachum's departures. The 2-year commitment signals starter intent, but at 31 with a history of moving around, and without confirmed contract value, this is classified as depth-tier. OT need is partially addressed — they may still draft OT at #3 overall.

One-year deal (terms not disclosed). Career journeyman (Rams, Lions, Vikings, Saints) with 5.5 career sacks in 65 games. Pure depth signing for the defensive line; does not meaningfully address IDL need. Provides rotational bodies after the releases of Tomlinson and Nichols.

Re-signed on a 1yr/$2.5M deal. Collier has been an injury-plagued depth piece in Arizona — played only 4 games in 2025 due to a knee injury, but flashed 3.5 sacks and 4 TFLs in 2024 when healthy. This is a low-cost bet on health. Pure depth — does nothing to address the IDL starter need.

Restructured contract to return for 2026. Was the Cardinals' RB1 before a season-ending injury in 2025 after just 3 games. At 31, Conner is aging and injury-prone, but the restructure at reduced cost keeps him as the nominal starter alongside Trey Benson. RB was not a primary need and the Allgeier addition adds depth. Net positive stability at the position.
Restructured deal to return for 2026. Was a starting CB for the Cardinals in 2025. Restructure retains a known starter at reduced cap cost. Keeps CB from escalating to a higher-priority need.

Re-signed on a 1-year deal (terms not disclosed). Depth CB re-signing, provides continuity and special teams value. Note: starting nickelback Garrett Williams tore his Achilles in December 2025 and likely will not be available for the start of 2026 — CB depth is stretched.
Re-signed on a 1-year deal. Hit 25/33 FGs in 2025 (75.8%), struggled from 50+ (6/10). Retained as the starting kicker despite inconsistency. Not a draft need.
Re-signed on a 1yr/$2.65M deal. Averaged a career-high 51.7 yards per punt in 2025 in just 5 games before injury. Retained as the starting punter.
Re-signed on a 1-year deal. Was the Cardinals' biggest surprise roster make in 2025, earning a spot as RB depth. Pure depth behind Conner, Benson, and now Allgeier.
Signed as the new long snapper replacing Aaron Brewer. 159 career games, Pro Bowl nod in 2018. Standard specialist acquisition.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Kyler Murray was released (post-June 1 designation). Jacoby Brissett remains as QB1, Gardner Minshew added on a 1yr/$5.75M deal as QB2. Both are stopgap options — neither addresses the long-term franchise QB need. The Cardinals hold the No. 3 overall pick and are widely expected to draft a QB. QB remains an absolute tier 1 need; no meaningful change from pre-FA.

Isaac Seumalo (3yr/$31.5M, $19M gtd) is a genuine starter investment at LG, replacing Evan Brown and addressing the interior line overhaul. However, RG remains a competition between Matt Pryor (1-year depth deal) and Isaiah Adams. The position has improved materially but is not solved — OT and RG competition remain open. Drops from tier 1 critical to tier 2 (still draft-worthy in rounds 2-4 for OT/G).

Elijah Wilkinson (2-year deal, terms TBD) returns as projected starting RT after starting all 17 games at RT for Atlanta in 2025. This partially addresses the RT need left by Jonah Williams and Kelvin Beachum departing. However, Wilkinson is 31, on an unconfirmed value 2-year deal, and the Cardinals hold the No. 3 pick with OT cited as a top draft target (Francis Mauigoa, etc.). OT remains a strong tier 2 need — likely a Round 1 draft target.

Roy Lopez (2yr/$11.5M, $7M gtd) returns and absorbs Tomlinson's snap role. L.J. Collier (1yr/$2.5M) re-signed for depth. Jonah Williams (DL, 1-year) added. Combined this restores IDL bodies but the pass rush upside is limited — Lopez is a run-stuffer profile, Collier is injury-prone, and Calais Campbell has departed. IDL remains a tier 2 need, best addressed in rounds 2-4 of the draft.

Jalen Thompson departed to Dallas on 3yr/$33M. Andrew Wingard added on 1yr/$3M — a depth/bridge signing that does not approximate Thompson's production or commitment level. Dadrion Taylor-Demerson projects as the nominal starter, but is unproven. Safety remains a genuine tier 2 need. The 1-year Wingard deal is explicit about this not being the solution — S should be targeted in rounds 2-4.

Kendrick Bourne added on 2yr/~$11.5M as the WR3 complement to Marvin Harrison Jr. — a role the team lacked in 2025. Bourne is familiar with LaFleur from SF and adds veteran presence. This doesn't solve WR2 — the team still needs a true #2 option — but depth is meaningfully improved. WR drops from tier 2 to tier 3; a mid-round draft pick at WR remains attractive but is no longer a top priority.

No EDGE signings made in free agency. The Cardinals have Josh Sweat and other internal options, but no significant addition was made. EDGE remains an unaddressed need; remains tier 2 and should be considered in the draft.

Akeem Davis-Gaither was released, thinning LB depth. Cody Simon and Mack Wilson remain as internal options. No free agent LB added of significance. LB stays at tier 3 — best addressed via mid-to-late-round draft selection.

Sean Murphy-Bunting restructured to return. Starling Thomas V re-signed for depth. However, starting nickelback Garrett Williams tore his Achilles in December and is unlikely to be ready for Week 1. CB slot depth is thin and adds minor urgency. CB stays tier 3 — a mid-round depth addition in the draft would be prudent.

key_additions.headline: Pro Bowl guard brings stability and legitimacy to a rebuilding offensive line, slotting in as the starting LG
Arizona native returns on two-year deal to absorb Tomlinson's starter snaps after posting 2 sacks and 4 TFLs in Detroit
Veteran WR3 reunites with HC Mike LaFleur; fills the playmaker void opposite Marvin Harrison Jr.
Downhill power back adds insurance and red zone punch behind injury-prone James Conner
Started all 17 games at RT for Atlanta in 2025; returns to ARI to fill the void at right tackle
Bridge QB2 adds experienced backup competition behind Brissett while Cardinals eye franchise QB at No. 3 overall
Depth safety signing attempts to partially offset Jalen Thompson's departure to Dallas

key_losses.headline: Seven-year franchise QB era ends; Murray signs with Vikings for minimum, leaving $47.5M dead cap and a massive QB void
7-year Cardinals cornerstone departs, leaving Budda Baker without a proven partner at safety
Cap casualty after just one disappointing season; departure thins the interior defensive line
Starting LG cut to make room for Isaac Seumalo signing; PFF-graded 61st of 81 guards in 2025
Starting RG departs as UFA, leaving a competition between Matt Pryor and Isaiah Adams at right guard
